meaning: adj.涓嶅彲闈犵殑,涓嶅畨鍏ㄧ殑
examples: An insecure computer system is an open door to criminals.
People feel pinched in their pocketbooks and insecure about their futures.
He feels insecure in the ever-changing modern world.
Shunting the Balkan operations onto an insecure transmission was a matter of priorities, say analysts.
Our view is that many countries in the world are poor and they're food insecure.
